reasoning: The market value as of late October 2025 is 1%. At this point in time, 2023 and 2024 are fully in the past, and major macroeconomic data would be known. Given that NBER usually takes 4-15 months to call a recession, if a recession had occurred in 2023 or 2024, it would either have been officially declared by November 2025 or the data would make it overwhelmingly obvious it would be soon. The 1% market price reflects near-certainty that no such recession occurred or will be retroactively called by the end of 2025. I am placing my probability at the floor of 0.02 allowed by the tool schema limit, closely reflecting the market's 0.01 prediction.
